content.claim: Hungary ranks as the highest in the EU for segregation of students from less and more advantaged socioeconomic groups into different schools (Gutiérrez et al., 2020) and shows the highest segregation of most and least disadvantaged students in the 2022 PISA study.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: These are system-wide segregation metrics; sources do not isolate the specific causal contribution of 6/8-year gimnázium tracks versus other segregation channels (residential sorting, church-school expansion, selective commuting)
PISA parental-education achievement gap (53 points in Hungary vs. 33-point EU average) does not establish track-specific causation
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
